title: Doctoral Programs
content:
Doctoral Programs

Application and Admission Requirements

Okan Homepage

Mechatronic Engineering

Application and Admission Requirements

MENU

Admission requirements are given below.

For applications to the Ph.D. program for master degree graduates:

Must have a bachelor's degree in Mechatronics, Mechanical, Automotive, Electrical, Electrical-Electronics, Electronics, Electronic and Communication, Control or Computer Engineering and must have completed a thesis/non-thesis master's degree in one of the above areas or (B) Admission decision for bachelors and master graduates of other disciplines will be given by the Program Admission Committee.

GRE (quantitative): at least 610 (old system), at least 149 (new system) or ALES (quantitative): at least 55 points or GMAT (Total): at least 450 points.

English Proficiency Exam Score (at least) YDS/KPDS/ÜDS: 55 points or TOEFL: 184 (CBT), 514 (PBT), 66 IBT)

At least one national/international refereed conference or journal paper.

For applications to the Ph.D. program for bachelor's degree graduates:

Must have a bachelor's degree in Mechatronics, Mechanical, Automotive, Electrical, Electrical-Electronics, Electronics, Electronic and Communication, Control or Computer Engineering or (B) Admission decision for bachelors degree graduates of other disciplines will be given by the Program Admission Committee.

GRE (quantitative): at least 685 (old system), 154 (new system) or ALES (quantitative): at least 70 points or GMAT (Total): at least 650 points.

English Proficiency Exam Score (at least) YDS/KPDS/ÜDS: 55 points or TOEFL: 184 (CBT), 514 (PBT), 66 IBT)

Bachelor's degree GPA of at least 3.00/4.00.

Applicants grades will be normalized according to the weights below, ranked, and placed depending on the program quota.

15 % of the GPA from the bachelor's or master's program used in the application

50 % of the ALES/GRE/GMAT score

15 % of the English proficiency exam score

20 % of the oral interview score

Required documents are listed below (all documents must be prepared in Turkish or English):

Original/color copy/color scan of

Bachelor's and/or master's degree diploma

Score sheet of ALES (quantitative) or GRE (quantitative) or GMAT (total) exam

English proficiency exam score sheet

Transcript (bachelor's and/or master's degree)

Pages from the passport that have photograph and identity information

At least two letters of recommendation (preferable from applicant's instructors and/or previous/current employers).

Statement of purpose

Three portrait pictures

Okan University graduate program application form

The applicants who do not have an ALES/GRE/GMAT exam score sheet may be accepted to the Ph.D. program on condition that he/she will pass the exam during his/her first year in the program as a special student. ALES results are valid for 3 years, and GRE/GMAT scores are valid for 5 years. TOEFL scores are valid for 2 years, whereas ÜDS/KPSD/YDS scores are valid for 3 years.
